_comment: Hand-curated layer of docs/capabilities.json (capability-config.md §2 p.3). The emitter (stapel_profiles/_capabilities.py) merges this over the derived axes; an axis present in conf.py DEFAULTS but missing here (or vice versa) is a loud emission error. Keep business_label/summary in business language — they surface in CTO conversations, not in code.
provides: User profiles and social graph: display name, avatar, locale/currency/measurement preferences, notification opt-outs, and follow/unfollow/block relationships between users, with a relationship-status lookup.

requires.reason: comm bus (events/functions), JWT authentication, GDPR provider registry, AppSettings config layer
consumes the user.registered / user.deleted comm events (OAuth avatar import, GDPR erasure on account deletion) that stapel-auth emits; decoupled via the comm bus, not a hard import dependency
avatar storage/existence checks (PROFILES_AVATAR_CHECK=comm calls cdn.media_exists) and OAuth avatar import (cdn.import_from_url) go through stapel_core.comm to a CDN provider
surfaces this module's registered GDPR provider (profile section export/erase) through DSR endpoints; the registration itself only needs stapel-core.gdpr

PROFILES_AVATAR_CHECK.summary: Controls how the profile service confirms an uploaded avatar reference actually exists on the CDN before saving it: through the internal comm bus (default), a legacy direct HTTP check, or skipped entirely (only the reference format is still validated).
